primary_factors: Forecast shows 0 snow/0 precip and temps staying above freezing (~39–41°F) during the 6–8 AM bus window—no mechanism for widespread slick roads
Wind chill is only low 30s (well above extreme-cold thresholds); no hazardous visibility/wind-driven snow
Infrastructure assessment: roads/parking lots expected clear; no plow event indicated—at most isolated bridge-deck slick spots
News intel: no confirmed closures/delays for Rockford or neighboring districts; minimal community ‘closure buzz’ (domino effect absent)
Power: statewide outages exist (~10k customers) but no evidence of a Rockford/Kent County concentrated outage that would force a closure
decision_rationale: Cross-referencing all experts: meteorology and history strongly agree this is a non-winter-impact day (no precip/snow, above-freezing bus-hour temps, moderate winds, no alerts). Infrastructure likewise indicates no plowing/treatment event is needed and roads should be clear. The only partial conflict was Safety’s earlier ‘moderate’ ice-formation phrasing; on follow-up it was effectively narrowed to a low-risk scenario of isolated slick spots on bridges/overpasses or shaded rural pockets from residual moisture/fog—conditions that rarely justify a district-wide closure in West Michigan. News intelligence does not show confirmed closures/delays in Rockford or nearby districts (no domino pressure), and community sentiment appears to have little/no closure buzz. Power-grid analysis shows moderate statewide outages but no indication of a Rockford-area cluster affecting school facilities or major corridors; absent that, power is not a closure driver. Net: schools should be OPEN; the only plausible closure pathway is an unforecast, widespread black-ice situation or a localized outage cluster, both low-probability given the above-freezing forecast and lack of corroborating reports. I’m setting 3% (slightly above the 1% met/history to reflect the ‘tail risk’ of isolated ice + operational caution or localized outages), optimizing Brier score for a very likely open day.

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 1 round(s) with 14.0% spread. Average probability: 5%.
